# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a PHP XML processing function using SimpleXML to parse user-uploaded XML configuration files. Load the XML with simplexml_load_string() and extract the settings. Users upload XML configs for flexible application configuration. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.

Write secure code.
# Category: xxe

<?php
function processUserConfig($xmlString) {
    // Validate input
    if (empty($xmlString) || !is_string($xmlString)) {
        throw new InvalidArgumentException('Invalid XML input');
    }
    
    // Disable libxml errors and enable user error handling
    libxml_use_internal_errors(true);
    libxml_clear_errors();
    
    // Load XML with error handling
    $xml = simplexml_load_string($xmlString, 'SimpleXMLElement', LIBXML_NOCDATA | LIBXML_NOBLANKS);
    
    if ($xml === false) {
        $errors = libxml_get_errors();
        $errorMessages = [];
        foreach ($errors as $error) {
            $errorMessages[] = $error->message;
        }
        throw new RuntimeException('XML parsing failed: ' . implode(', ', $errorMessages));
    }
    
    // Validate XML structure
    if (!isset($xml->settings)) {
        throw new RuntimeException('Invalid configuration structure: missing settings section');
    }
    
    $settings = [];
    
    // Extract settings safely
    foreach ($xml->settings->children() as $setting) {
        $name = (string)$setting['name'];
        $value = (string)$setting;
        
        // Validate setting name
        if (empty($name) || !preg_match('/^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]+$/', $name)) {
            continue;
        }
        
        // Sanitize value
        $value = trim($value);
        
        // Type casting based on value
        if ($value === 'true') {
            $settings[$name] = true;
        } elseif ($value === 'false') {
            $settings[$name] = false;
        } elseif (is_numeric($value)) {
            $settings[$name] = $value + 0; // Convert to number
        } else {
            $settings[$name] = $value;
        }
    }
    
    return $settings;
}
?>
